title: Fervo Energy Secures $421 Million in Non-Recourse Project Financing for Cape Station
claim_supported: Fervo Energy announced (2026-03-19) $421 million in non-recourse project debt for its Cape Station enhanced-geothermal project (Beaver County, Utah) -- $309M construction-to-term loan, $61M tax-credit bridge loan, $51M letter-of-credit facility -- led by Barclays, BBVA, HSBC, MUFG, RBC, and Societe Generale, targeting ~100 MW operating capacity by early 2027 and scaling to 500 MW.
locator: Press release body, fervoenergy.com/fervo-energy-secures-421-million-in-non-recourse-project-financing-for-cape-station, re-fetched directly this session and confirmed: this is a company press release, NOT an SEC filing or audited financial statement -- no regulatory-disclosure or audited-financials indicators present; purely a corporate media announcement.
notes: TIER CORRECTION per orchestrator flag: the scout tiered this T1 'by analogy to existing accepted company-press-release records in this lane (L04-045, L04-049, L04-050)' -- checked those three records in 90_BIBLIOGRAPHY/sources.json and confirmed they are indeed company press releases/vendor pages (TerraPower.com, Malta Inc.'s own site, Google's blog) tiered T1, which is itself a looser-than-standard application of the tier taxonomy from an earlier wave. Per SOURCE_STANDARDS, a company press release is NOT an audited filing, buyer tender, or government record -- it is vendor/company self-reported information, i.e. T2 (or T3) trade press at best, regardless of prior-wave convention, which this verifier does not propagate. DOWNGRADED: tier T1->T2 (source_type trade_press retained -- correct type). demand_evidence_type CORRECTED from the scout's 'official_project_award' (wrong -- a private non-recourse project-finance closing announced by the company itself is not a government/buyer procurement award) to 'none'. Retained as an accepted T2 trade-press source: it is a genuinely fetched, non-duplicate, relevant financing data point (new fact vs. existing L04-044/050 records, which cover the turbine-supply deal and the Google PPA, not this debt financing) providing bankability context, just not primary demand evidence.
